# Ant Code v1.0 Acceptance

Date: 2026-04-28
Package: `@ant-code/cli@1.0.0`
Public CLI: `ant-code`
Internal compatibility alias: `lab-agent`

This document records the acceptance posture for the Ant Code v1.0 internal lab release.

## Go Decision

Ant Code v1.0 is accepted for controlled internal lab distribution from the local clean-room repository.

The accepted scope is a local-first coding agent with:

- local file, git, shell, workflow, memory, and MCP tool execution
- explicit local approvals for writes and mutating shell commands
- OpenAI Chat Completions compatible model-adapter support
- no direct provider credentials in the local client
- no remote tool server dependency
- local bounded session metadata with high-sensitivity zero-retention support
- clean-room provenance, dependency, endpoint, and release-seal evidence
- packed internal release dependency graph pinned by `npm-shrinkwrap.json`
- Ink/React terminal UI with conversation-first transcript, side panels, Inspector history, and file-by-file patch browsing

## Verification Evidence

Required release verification passed:

```powershell
npm run verify:release
```

Observed result:

- syntax check passed for 81 source files
- forbidden endpoint scan passed
- provenance check passed for 14 source modules
- dependency policy check passed for 2 reviewed runtime dependencies
- package lock and npm shrinkwrap dependency graphs match
- unit and integration tests passed: 145 passed, 0 failed
- install readiness passed
- model gateway compatibility mock check passed
- readiness documentation check passed
- release seal check passed
- audit artifacts regenerated

## Model Adapter Smoke Test

The local adapter was validated with:

- protocol: `openai-chat`
- URL shape: `http://localhost:8080/v1/chat/completions`
- model: `claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929`
- network mode: `offline`

Observed smoke result:

```text
sonnet-ready
```

No adapter access token is recorded in this repository or in this document.

## Startup

From the local checkout:

```powershell
cd C:\saveproject\LBJ-workspace\lab-agent
node .\src\cli\index.js tui
```

For a new PowerShell window, Windows user environment variables can provide the local adapter configuration. For an already-open PowerShell window, refresh them into the process before starting:

```powershell
'LAB_MODEL_GATEWAY_PROTOCOL','LAB_MODEL_GATEWAY_URL','LAB_MODEL_GATEWAY_API_KEY','LAB_AGENT_MODEL','LAB_AGENT_NETWORK_MODE' | ForEach-Object {
  $v = [Environment]::GetEnvironmentVariable($_, 'User')
  if ($v) { Set-Item "Env:\$_" $v }
}
node .\src\cli\index.js tui
```

## Security Boundary

Accepted v1.0 boundary:

- Model traffic uses only the configured model gateway/model adapter endpoint.
- Adapter authentication is local gateway access control, not a provider credential pass-through.
- Provider credentials remain inside the gateway/model adapter service boundary.
- Tool calls returned by the model are executed by the local Ant Code permission engine.
- Write and mutating shell tools require explicit local approval unless a session flag grants that approval.
- MCP servers are explicit local stdio processes only.
- Session metadata remains local, bounded, and configurable for zero-retention high-sensitivity projects.

## Known Limitations

These limitations are accepted for v1.0 internal use:

- The TUI does not yet include advanced editor panes or a full visual file tree.
- Live gateway health URL may be unset on a single workstation adapter; broad rollout should configure `LAB_MODEL_GATEWAY_HEALTH_URL`.
- Default metadata policy is standard local retention; sensitive research projects should set `LAB_AGENT_SENSITIVITY=high`.
- The package is private and intended for local checkout or lab-managed distribution, not public npm publishing.
- Remote tools, cloud sessions, plugin marketplace discovery, and transcript upload are intentionally out of scope.

## Rollback

Disable model turns without removing the local client:

```powershell
Remove-Item Env:\LAB_MODEL_GATEWAY_URL -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
Remove-Item Env:\LAB_MODEL_GATEWAY_HEALTH_URL -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
Remove-Item Env:\LAB_MODEL_GATEWAY_API_KEY -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
$env:LAB_AGENT_NETWORK_MODE = "offline"
```

Local slash commands, diagnostics, and metadata cleanup remain available after model access is disabled.

## Maintainer Notes

- Public identity is Ant Code.
- Internal config and protocol identifiers may continue to use `lab-agent` for compatibility.
- The legacy contaminated repository remains prohibited as an implementation source.
- Future external distribution requires a separate policy review and a fresh release seal.
